question: [File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: cacheKeyGenerator()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the CACHE_KEY_VERSION_SUFFIX='-v1' appended by cacheKeyGenerator() to predict the exact Redis key for any block endpoint (since MD5 of a known URL is deterministic), and use this knowledge to time cache-poisoning attacks that replace fresh block data with stale data at the moment of cache expiry, delaying block visibility by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: responseCacheUpdateHandler()] Can an unprivileged user send a high volume of requests to unique block-related URLs (e.g., /api/v1/blocks?limit=1&timestamp=<unique>) so that responseCacheUpdateHandler() stores a unique Redis key for each, exhausting Redis memory (maxmemory), causing eviction of legitimate block cache entries and forcing cache misses that delay fresh block data delivery by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: responseCacheCheckHandler()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of request rate limiting in responseCacheCheckHandler() to send a cache-busting flood of requests with unique URLs to block endpoints, preventing the cache from ever being populated with fresh block data and causing all clients to receive delayed block information for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getCacheControlExpiryOrDefault()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the greedy regex CACHE_CONTROL_REGEX=/^.*max-age=(\\d+)/ by causing a response with a Cache-Control header like 'no-store, max-age=99999' to be parsed, causing getCacheControlExpiryOrDefault() to extract 99999 as the TTL and store a stale block response in Redis for 99999 seconds (far exceeding 500% of average block time), freezing visible block state?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: responseCacheUpdateHandler()] Can an unprivileged user trigger a 304 response path in responseCacheUpdateHandler() where isUnmodified=true, causing the headers to be reconstructed from config.response.headers.default merged with res.getHeaders() and res.locals[responseHeadersLabel], and if res.locals[responseHeadersLabel] contains attacker-influenced values, inject a stale ETag into the cached block response that persists for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: responseCacheCheckHandler()] Can an unprivileged user send concurrent requests to the same block endpoint at the exact moment the cache entry expires (TTL=0), causing multiple simultaneous cache misses in responseCacheCheckHandler() (since getSingleWithTtl returns undefined for all), triggering a thundering herd that overwhelms the upstream block data source and delays cache repopulation by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: negotiate()] Can an unprivileged user send a request with Accept-Encoding: gzip to a block endpoint where cachedResponse.compressed=false, causing negotiate() to skip the gzip branch and serve the uncompressed body with an incorrect Content-Length (getUncompressedLength()), causing client-side parsing errors that prevent clients from reading fresh block data and effectively freezing their view of block state for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: responseCacheCheckHandler()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that responseCacheCheckHandler() spreads cachedResponse.headers into the response headers without sanitization (`...cachedResponse.headers`), and if a stale cached block response contains attacker-influenced headers (e.g., a large max-age), cause downstream clients to cache the stale block response for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: cacheKeyGenerator()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that cacheKeyGenerator() uses req.originalUrl (which includes query parameters) without normalization, so that /api/v1/blocks?limit=1 and /api/v1/blocks?limit=1& produce different MD5 hashes, causing cache fragmentation that prevents any single block endpoint from being consistently cached and delays fresh block data delivery by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: responseCacheUpdateHandler()] Can an unprivileged user send requests that cause responseCacheUpdateHandler() to call getCache().setSingle() with ttl=0 (when getCacheControlExpiryOrDefault returns 0), which is blocked by the `if (ttl > 0)` guard, causing the cache to never be populated for that block endpoint and forcing all clients to always receive potentially stale upstream data, delaying block visibility by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: responseCacheCheckHandler()] Can an unprivileged user send a HEAD request to a block endpoint where the cached response is compressed (cachedResponse.compressed=true), causing responseCacheCheckHandler() to call negotiate() which sets Content-Encoding and Content-Length headers but sends no body, causing HTTP clients to hang waiting for a body that never arrives and freezing their view of block state for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: responseCacheCheckHandler()] Can an unprivileged user send requests with an extremely long if-none-match header value to responseCacheCheckHandler(), causing the string comparison `conditionalHeader === cachedResponse.headers[ETAG_HEADER]` to consume excessive CPU time and delay the response for block endpoints by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: responseCacheCheckHandler()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the elapsed time is computed as `Date.now() - startTime` where startTime comes from `res.locals[requestStartTime] || Date.now()`, and by manipulating the request timing, cause the logged elapsed time to be misleading, masking a slow cache response that delays block data delivery by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: responseCacheUpdateHandler()] Can an unprivileged user send requests that cause responseCacheUpdateHandler() to be called without a corresponding responseCacheCheckHandler() having set res.locals[responseCacheKeyLabel], causing responseCacheKey to be undefined and the cache update to be silently skipped, leaving block endpoints uncached and serving stale data for 500%+ of average block time?
